key_insight: A fully differentiable, matrix-free PyTorch phase-field fracture solver can run on CPUs and GPUs without custom compiled extensions while supporting both forward simulation and inverse parameter identification.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable fracture simulation and gradient-based calibration for solid mechanics workflows, with direct compatibility for optimization and machine-learning pipelines.
method: Element-wise finite-element tensor contractions with scatter-based accumulation replace global sparse stiffness assembly in explicit dynamics.
Supports AT1/AT2 phase-field formulations, multiple energy splits, and plane strain/plane stress settings.
Uses PyTorch autograd for explicit kernels and a custom implicit backward rule for the bound-constrained damage solve via CG-based differentiation.
evidence_signal: The solver was benchmarked on four dynamic and two quasi-static fracture cases and recovered fracture energy $G_c$ with relative error below 10^-3 in a few L-BFGS steps for glass and alumina.
watch_item: Performance and scalability depend on careful GPU memory management and CG convergence in the implicit damage solve, especially for very large meshes.
paper_title: A matrix-free, differentiable PyTorch solver for phase-field fracture: Formulation, benchmarks, and inverse analysis
